hook.text: It takes 9 years to get diagnosed with endometriosis. Here is why.
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: shock at the long delay, urgency, frustration
hook.target_audience_raw: people with endometriosis symptoms, undiagnosed individuals, women with period pain

main_claim.text: The average time to get an endometriosis diagnosis in the UK is 9 years due to multiple factors: social and clinical normalization of period pain, lack of education, misdiagnosis as IBS, communication barriers in short GP appointments, and varied symptom presentation.

supporting_explanation.summary: Liz Bruen, an Endometriosis Clinical Nurse Specialist, explains that period issues are often dismissed as normal, teenagers struggle to articulate symptoms, and adults become exasperated. Endometriosis can mimic IBS, and the condition affects people differently. She advises tracking symptoms and bringing a record to appointments to improve communication.

funnel_rationale: Provides general education on diagnosis delay and symptom awareness, not specific to a clinic or booking.
